text:
We introduce Video-LLaMA, a multi-modal framework designed to bridge the gap between Large Language Models (LLMs) and video-centric understanding by integrating both visual and auditory comprehension. Our approach utilizes a instruction-tuning strategy that bootstraps cross-modal alignment from frozen, pre-trained visual and audio encoders. Unlike existing models that often focus on a single sensory modality, Video-LLaMA addresses two critical hurdles in video analysis: capturing temporal dynamics within visual scenes and effectively integrating synchronized audio-visual signals.

To address temporal modeling, we propose a Video Q-former that adapts a pre-trained image encoder for video sequences, utilizing a video-to-text generation task to establish robust video-language correspondence. To handle auditory data, we leverage ImageBind as a universal backbone and introduce an Audio Q-former to map complex acoustic signals into the LLM’s embedding space. The model is trained through a multi-stage process, beginning with large-scale alignment on massive video-captioning datasets, followed by fine-tuning on high-quality visual instruction data. Experimental results demonstrate that Video-LLaMA can effectively perceive complex video content and generate coherent, contextually grounded responses based on both what it "sees" and "hears."
